rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ENGELBERG VS. ELLEN TSANG, AS TRUSTEE OF THE ARZA TRUST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Cross-Defendant To Compel Additional Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 27, 2022 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT CHARLES ENGELBERG Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Cross-Defendant To Compel Additional Deposition is transferred to department 301 to be heard on the discovery calendar at 9:00 a.m. on February 9, 2022 per SFLR 8.10. Parties to e-mail courtesy copies of their respective filings to department 301 at Discovery302@sftc.org no later than January 27, 2021 by 5:00 p.m. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, VS. THERESA LYNNE MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 27, 2022 line . CROSS DEFENDANT THERESA PHINNESSEE MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is GRANTED. Default on the non-operative First Amened Cross-Complaint is set aside. Second Amended Cross-Complaint was filed and answered by the moving defendant. The Second Amended Cross-Complaint is not yet at issue and there is no trial date set. Potential uncertainty as to substantive changes between original cross-complaint (answered), First Amended Cross-Complaint and Second Amended Cross-Complaint (answered), combined with the uncertain status of the requests for default (under review for months between submission and processing) satisfies the CCP 473(b) requirements. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOANNA L. PFEISTER VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association'S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 27, 2022 line 5. Defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association'S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action as to the moving defendant. Plaintiff shall be mindful of applicability of CC 47 and not base any of the causes of action on privileged activity. All amendments shall be in bold font. The Court notes the objection made by Plaintiff as to the form of the motion filing (notice, demurrer and points and authorities being one document). The Court finds no prejudice in the filing of the combined document at this time. However, moving party shall comply with all CCP and CRC procedural requirements in the future to avoid unnecessary and preventable procedural issues. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOANNA L. PFEISTER VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Rendering Judgment By Default Against Defendant Pursuant To Ccp 2023.030(D)(4) For Disobeying The Court'S Orders And Ccop 2023.010(D) Through 2023.010(I)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 27, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFF JOANNA PFEISTER Motion For Order Rendering Judgment By Default Against Defendant Pursuant To Ccp 2023.030(D)(4) For Disobeying The Court'S Orders And Ccop 2023.010(D) Through 2023.010(I) is DENIED. Plaintiff is seeking terminating sanctions, a drastic remedy not available at this stage of the litigation and under the facts of this case. Plaintiff shall engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts regarding discovery disputes, including those arising out of the Court's prior orders, and may bring further motions to compel if necessary and proper. All discovery matters shall be referred to discovery referee per December 13, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
